body:
{
  "title": "Anchor Board Meeting Q1 2027",
  "compiled_truth": "The Q1 2027 board meeting for [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) convened on April 15th at the company's main office, running about three hours with a working lunch. Attendees included CEO [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss), CTO [Ora Yamamoto](people/ora-yamamoto), board member [Bex Andersen](people/bex-andersen), VP of Engineering [Piper Bergmann](people/piper-bergmann), and head of go-to-market [Dani Osei](people/dani-osei). The two main agenda items were the enterprise pipeline review and a discussion around scaling the team from the current 14 headcount to 20 by end of Q2.\n\nSeren opened with a revenue update. ARR had crossed $1.1M, driven primarily by three enterprise accounts that signed in late Q4 2026 and early Q1 2027. The pipeline looked healthy — Dani walked through 11 active enterprise opportunities, 4 of which were in late-stage procurement. Average deal size was trending upward, which [Dani Osei](people/dani-osei) attributed to Anchor's positioning as deterministic data infrastructure rather than a point solution. Bex pushed back on the sales cycle length, noting that two of the largest deals had been in pipeline for over 90 days with no clear close date. Dani acknowledged this and proposed bringing in a solutions engineer dedicated to enterprise POCs to accelerate technical validation.\n\n[Piper Bergmann](people/piper-bergmann) presented the hiring plan. Six roles were open: two senior backend engineers, one DevRel hire, one solutions engineer (the role Dani had flagged), one infrastructure SRE, and one technical writer. Ora noted that the single-pass primitves work was at a critical juncture and that the two backend hires needed to be senior enough to contribute to core protocol work within their first quarter. Bex asked about burn rate implications — Seren confirmed the company had approximately 16 months of runway at current spend, dropping to ~11 months if all six hires were made on schedule. The board discussed whether to stagger hires or raise a bridge round. No resolution was reached; Seren committed to presenting a scenario analysis at the next board sync.\n\nOne smaller but notable discussion: Ora raised concerns about a potential IP overlap flagged during a customer's legal review. The issue was deemed low-risk by outside counsel but the board agreed to commission a more thorough freedom-to-operate analysis. Bex volunteered to connect Anchor with a firm she'd used previously.\n\nMeeting adjourned at 2:15 PM. Next board meeting scheduled for July.",
  "timeline": [
    "- **2026-09-12** | [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) closes seed round; board formally constituted with [Bex Andersen](people/bex-andersen) joining as independent director.",
    "- **2026-11-30** | First enterprise contract signed, marking Anchor's shift from developer-tool positioning to infrastructure sales.",
    "- **2027-01-18** | [Piper Bergmann](people/piper-bergmann) promoted to VP of Engineering; begins building out formal hiring pipeline.",
    "- **2027-03-03** | [Dani Osei](people/dani-osei) presents initial enterprise pipeline deck to leadership team — 11 active opps identified.",
    "- **2027-04-15** | Q1 2027 board meeting held. Enterprise pipeline and team growth to 20 discussed in detail.",
    "- **2027-05-01** | Freedom-to-operate legal analysis kicked off following board directive.",
    "- **2027-06-09** | 17th employee starts — solutions engineer role filled, dedicated to enterprise POC support.",
    "- **2027-07-22** | Q2 2027 board meeting. [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) presents runway scenario analysis and bridge round options.",
    "- **2027-10-14** | Anchor headcount reaches 21, slightly ahead of the growth target discussed at the April board meeting."
  ]
}
